sourceRef.citation: Kourovka Notebook, Problem 17.112 (Khukhro & Mazurov eds., ed.17)

statement.latex: A subgroup$A$of a group$G$is said to be$G$-permutable in$G$if for every subgroup$B$of$G$there exists an element$x G$such that$AB^x=B^xA$. A subgroup$A$is said to be hereditarily$G$-permutable in$G$if$A$is$E$-permutable in every subgroup$E$of$G$containing$A$. Which finite non-abelian simple groups$G$possess a) a non-trivial$G$-permutable subgroup? b) a non-trivial hereditarily$G$-per\-mu\-table sub...

reiAssessment.solveRationale: Group theory I_INFINITE_SEARCH; Rei not specialized in group theory (yet)
reiAssessment.formalizationNotes: Kourovka problems are typically research-level group theory
